title: cyperus impubes var. rohlfsii
intro: Cyperus impubes var. rohlfsii, also known as dwarf papyrus or Rohlfs' sedge, is a plant species in the sedge family, characterized by its small, yellowish-green flowers and tubers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cyperus impubes var. rohlfsii include soothing digestive discomfort, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
